source_old_req: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION thread and its implementation ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL must operate within a periodic dispatching protocol with a period of 80 ms and priority level 1. The thread shall assume that the input data ports acc_in_data_x, acc_in_data_y, acc_in_data_z, mag_in_data_x, mag_in_data_y, and mag_in_data_z contain valid floating-point values of type AHRS::Data_Types::FLOAT32_TYPE_AADL. The thread shall guarantee that the output data ports gyro_in_data_x, gyro_in_data_y, and gyro_in_data_z are determined exclusively by the current values on the input ports and internal computations performed by the ATTITUDE_CALCULATION thread. Additionally, the thread shall guarantee that all state updates maintain the data type constraints of the port definitions, ensuring no violations occur in the values propagated through the gyro_in_data_x, gyro_in_data_y, and gyro_in_data_z ports.
natural_requirement: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL. In the implementation layer, ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL shall route data flow from acc_in_data_x to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_x through ACC_PC1 and data flow from acc_in_data_y to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_y through ACC_PC2.

requirement_items.text: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from acc_in_data_x to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_x through connection ACC_PC1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC1 from acc_in_data_x to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_x.

requirement_items.text: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from acc_in_data_y to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_y through connection ACC_PC2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC2 from acc_in_data_y to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_y.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC1 from acc_in_data_x to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_x. The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall declare connection ACC_PC2 from acc_in_data_y to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_y.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from acc_in_data_x to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_x through connection ACC_PC1. The ATTITUDE_CALCULATION.IMPL implementation shall route the data flow from acc_in_data_y to attitude_sub.acc_in_data_y through connection ACC_PC2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
